requirement: each rule is observable or testable

findings.observation: Conformance Classes define observable behavior for documents, parsers, linters, evaluators, and report renderers, each phrased as a checkable acceptance/rejection criterion.
Structural requirements (assessment must be a single non-empty scalar; direct-target requirements must declare resolvable factors) map to observable file properties a linter can decide.
Some roll-up requirements are softer ('SHOULD include a rationale'), but they are recommendations, not normative obligations, so they do not undermine decidable conformance.

rationale: Each normative rule maps to something a reader or tool can observe or test about a file or implementation, so independent readers decide conformance the same way. Satisfies the requirement.
